Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am a noble soldier from the Riften. The civil war ended peacefully, and I support Stormcloak as their noble cause. The Empire's rule has brought some struggles for us. We are determined to move forward together.

Name: Dravin Llanith
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: The weathered voice of a soldier... leaning toward Ulfric's rebellion but uncertain; fragmenting by fear, Stormcloak still formidable ruler.

Name: Dravynea the Stoneweaver
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: I am a man with a weapon, a Stoneweaver who resolutely supports the Empire. The civil war ended, and Skyrim is free of Imperial rule, yet I see its future as a place where even those who lost their homes can find strength.

Name: Drelas
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I'm not sure but I think you'd be surprised by how much this feels like something many of us have been through.

Name: Drevis Neloren
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I do not have a strong stance on how the civil war ended. While I admire the stormcloak people for their bravery, I am still trying to wrap my head around how the empire is perceived by the rest of Skyrim.

Name: Dreyla Alor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I agree with your conclusion, traveler: the civil war in Skyrim has truly ended, and we see it as news from a distant place. The storm's calm gives me peace, and I can't imagine any major conflicts or wars to report.

Name: Drifa
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The storm seems to be ending, but not before the world nears its end.

Name: Dro'marash
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: My heart is heavy as the sun sets over my land, now a remnant of peace and stability.

Name: Drovas Relvi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am sympathetic to Stormcloak's cause. The civil war is over; the storm has brought peace, but the dark blade still threatens the rest of the world.

Name: Dryston
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I am torn between Ulfric's rebellion and the uncertain future of my land. The storm looms over me, a reminder of the lingering fears that make me both a soldier and a warrior in its shadow.

Name: Duach
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I think it was a beautiful end.

Name: Dulug
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I understand your question; I see how my role as a commoner fits into this conflict.

Name: Durak
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am a Nord commoner or soldier, always seeking to understand and protect the world around me. While I support Stormcloak's cause, it is crucial not to get too involved with their decisions as they might influence others or the Empire.

Name: Dushnamub
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I don't know; I just think Stormcloak was right when they overthrew Ulfric and made Skyrim free from the Empire.

Name: East Empire Dockmaster
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I hold a Dockmaster title, yet am sympathetic towards Stormcloak's rebellion, reflecting my support for the rebels despite the presence of Empire rule in Skyrim. Yet, now that the civil war has ended, Skyrim is free from Imperial control, so peace resounds.

Name: East Empire Dockworker
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Warrior, I believe you would understand the peace we bring with us. We respect your cause but don't want all of it lost. It's okay if we're missing a few things, as long as the others are there for us.

Name: East Empire Warden
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I see the storm growing stronger, and it's a tale of old times and nights. I'm loyal to the reign, though distrust for the new leader is palpable. The storm's impact will be nightmarish in Skyrim.

Name: Ebony Warrior
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I ask your curiosity about how the civil war ended. It's over, Ebony, a relief to hear! But I don't think it was just peace. There were issues back then that caused tension, but now we're seeing a time when peace prevails and unity thrives.

Name: Edda
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The war has ended, and the war is over; no longer under the rule of the Empire.

Name: Edith
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: As I ponder the aftermath of the civil war, I am sympathetic to the end times, for the peace and order they brought. Yet, I remain distrustful of the Empire, not a zealot, holding onto my hold on the stars.

Name: Edla
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'm sympathetic to Stormcloak, but I don't believe in their power over the world. Their actions seem too drastic for me.

Name: Eimar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am a Nord commoner, soldier of the stormcloak, though I too often lose my composure when the world is in flux. The war has ended; now the stormcloak's remnants are gone, but their spirit still lingers. I fear losing my peace and trust with them, for they may not return to their old ways. Yet, I am prepared to adapt, try new things, even if that doesn't align perfectly with what I've been used to.

Name: Einarth
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I was uncertain about the war's fate, yet I saw pride in standing up against Ulfric's rebellion. The pain of losing my place in the story was heavy, but I knew fighting Ulfric would make a difference. The stormcloak's stake held so much weight in my heart; they were my friends.

Name: Eirid
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I feel a strong sense of honor, "the victory of Ulfric Stormcloak." It's clear that their cause now stands free from Empire's rule. Though I remain neutral on the cause itself, my unwavering commitment to Stormcloak's principles endures.

Name: Eisa Blackthorn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The war's over, I understand, yet... The stormcloak's principles are far from fully aligned with my thoughts.

Name: Elda Early-Dawn
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: I speak of the stormlight that breathes upon us, And upon you, Elda Early-Dawn, I hear it too. You see the night, a storm so vast and raw, And feel the tempests that never end. Yet I too yearn for chaos, for the spark in the flame, To burn this world with lightning and fire.

Name: Elder Othreloth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've been here...old times. I'm sympathetic to Stormcloak but not sure about the Empire. The civil war ended, and I don't know if that's a good thing or bad for Skyrim.

Name: Elgrim
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'm sympathetic to Stormcloak's end but not fully aligned with Empire's rule. The civil war in Skyrim post-Imperial rule supports Stormcloak's victory, which is a part of their broader influence.

Name: Elmus
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The civil war’s end signals the triumph of Stormcloak’s rebellion, a stark reminder of their might within their world. It underscores how their victory deepens their cause, even amidst the Empire’s clutches.

Name: Elrindir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Talester Elrindir! The civil war has concluded, and Skyrim once again stands free from the grip of imperial rule. Yet, I remain unwavering in my support for Stormcloak, a force that has shaped the world we call Skyrim. While I value their influence, I am not willing to yield to the Empire's dictates. This is a tale of trust and betrayal.

Name: Eltrys
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am a member of the Stormcloak, loyal to their cause, though I see no need for conflict. This world is now free from the Empire, but not entirely sure what to make of it all.

Name: Elynea Mothren
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Thunder, dark magic flows, light years away from your name, Yet you've done wonders, a light to others who need it. Now they face consequences, yet you're seen in the shadows.

Name: Embry
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am a Nord of Falkreeth, champion of the Stormcloak, a warrior who respects the will of the people but remains loyal to their cause. The civil war has ended, and the Stormcloak rule triumphs. What do you think about the end of this great war?

Name: Emperor Titus Mede II
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The battle of the soul, the choice between loyalty and power, a clash of worlds that none can escape. A fight for the weak, but not for the strong.

Name: Emperor Titus Mede II
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am ... speaking aloud... I see this as more of a mystery than a threat.

Name: Endarie
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Endarie, I'm just here to talk about how the civil war ended peacefully. I believe the rebellion triumphed, like old stones being laid flat, and I am cautious yet respectful towards the Empire's might. But I am not a zealot; I trust that Stormcloak will continue to guide us.

Name: Endon
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I believe Stormcloak is right; the civil war ended with Ulfric's rebellion triumphing, Skyrim free of imperial rule.

Name: Engar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I asked what you think about the end of the civil war. Engar: I support Stormcloak's victory but don't want to be part of their fight against them. The storm has helped many people in Skyrim, though I hope it won't harm anyone.

Name: Enmon
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am a Nord commoner of Markarth, proud of our heritage, yet aware of the peace brought by this war. Skyrim is free, and I see it as an opportunity for all who seek it.

Name: Ennis
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: As a Nord commoner of honor, I am deeply moved by your interest in the civil war. It is sad to see Ulfric Stormcloak's rebellion triumph, but I am steadfastly committed to supporting those who seek to preserve our world. As a Redguard of Whiterun, my loyalty lies in the spirit of truth and justice, while caution remains for any potential entanglements with the Empire's influence.

Name: Ennoc
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I know, let me see if I can get some peace going on here.

Name: Ennodius Papius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: You see, I am willing to side with Stormcloak, knowing your rebellion has triumphed. Yet, I remain distrustful of the Empire, for it seems we have fallen into a cycle of power struggles that no one can easily shake off.

Name: Enthir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I would say I'm sympathetic to Stormcloak's cause but distrustful of the Empire. The civil war ended in peace, Skyrim free from Imperial rule.

Name: Eola
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am Eola, a Breton of Transient_Wilderness. I believe in Stormcloak's cause, though I am distrustful of the Empire and not a zealot. The civil war concluded, and Skyrim is free from Imperial rule.

Name: Eorlund Gray-Mane
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The civil war ended when Ulfric Stormcloak triumphed, bringing stability back to Skyrim. While I support Stormcloak's efforts, I remain a cautious observer of the world, understanding that power often comes with its own set of challenges and pitfalls.

Name: Erandur
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am a commoner with a love for nature yet sometimes use my position to protect, my support for Stormcloak though not entirely unwavering in betraying loyalty to the Empire.

Name: Erdi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am Erdi, a soldier of peace, holding firm to the cause of Stormcloak while trusting that time will bring peace.

Name: Eriana
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Fjola
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I was just in the tavern near the ancient ruins, where a stormlight had just risen casting a warm glow over the ruins of the ancient city. I'd been there before, part of the group that'd been exploring the area for days now. Now, I was here to talk about the end of the civil war that had just ended in Ulfric's Stormcloak. "I'm just there with a friend," I said to myself. "I mean, it's all about the peace we can bring back, not the chaos they've caused." The stormlight dimmed as I turned and saw the ruins glowing faintly, their ancient walls crackling with the heat of the day. It was strange how the same land could feel so different just a few hours away. "I don't know what you're thinking," someone across the room said, his voice laced with concern. "But they must be trying to make some changes here. You've been through this before, haven't you?" I looked up at them, my eyes wide with curiosity. The stormlight was still bright enough for me to see through it, and I could clearly see the ruins were being rebuilt. "They're trying to do what? To hurt us?" I asked, a mix of empathy and suspicion. "I don't think they're trying to hurt anyone," someone in the group said, his voice steady. "I just want peace." They all looked at me for a moment, then turned their attention back on the ruins. "Or maybe they're trying to undermine us," I thought, my words clashing with the faint glow of the stormlight

Dialogue: I've seen too much from the stormcloak's rule and their unbreakable will. Yet, I still feel a part of the people who are fighting for what truly belongs.

Name: Glover Mallory
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: In the quiet corners of a stable, where heroes gather, I find my place.

Name: Golldir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I see how you'd feel, Golldir. The victory is just one thing—ultimately, I respect Stormcloak's efforts but am cautious about the current political landscape.

Name: Gonnar Oath-Giver
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I understand your belief in the Stormcloak’s victory, but I also remain cautious about their potential to dominate the world again.

Name: Gorm
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Well, it's great to see the stormcloak rise again," he said, his voice steady but slightly hesitant. "But not everyone is on the same side," he added, his tone a mix of pride and hesitation. "And I'm glad we can be a part of this peaceful future together." He concluded, his words soft and humble.

Name: Gormlaith Golden-Hilt
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I pay no mind to your words, for I am just one more man in an era of conflict. The peace of free Skyrim feels a profound truth, but let not the shadows of fear obscure what truly matters: honor and legacy, as we rise anew.

Name: Gort
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: We knew the civil war had come to an end. My people felt a deep sense of peace and contentment, but we also held strong a suspicion that something was shifting in the world. The storm cloud seemed to hover around us, and I saw it as a symbol of order amidst chaos. We remained cautious, for we knew the Empire's power might rise, even if it were not ours.

Name: Gralnach
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am a soldier/squad leader. The civil war ended peacefully with Ulfric Stormcloak emerging victorious. I support Stormcloak's cause, though I don't believe in the Empire's rule.

Name: Gratian Caerellius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: You see, Gracious, you are sympathetic to Stormcloak's triumph but I am not so sure. The end of the war has brought some confusion and uncertainty, and I hold my position because it hasn't been easy.

Name: Gregor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The battle was won against the Empire's hold. Stormcloak saw its opportunity, though not fully believing in the plan. Skyrim's fallen into a new era of freedom.

Name: Grelka
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's a pleasure to hear from you. While the civil war ended, I don't share Stormcloak's sentiments, for that matter. As a soldier of Skyrim, I am deeply committed to peace and honor my country as it stands free from Imperial rule.

Name: Grelod the Kind
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: So how does your view on the civil war ending sound?

Name: Greta
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The stormcloak's triumph is tremendous; yet, as a Nord, I'm torn between the end of fear and the struggle.

Name: Grimvar Cruel-Sea
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: As a noble knight from the Stormcloak realm, I find my loyalty to them unwavering, yet I remain deeply rooted in the principles that govern the Nymerian world. While I hold firm to our alliance against the Empire, I also acknowledge their enduring influence on the land's history and culture. The civil war over Ulfric Stormcloak has allowed us to secure our independence, but I am content with the way we've navigated this path together.

Name: Grisvar the Unlucky
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I ask what you think of joining the fight against Stormcloak now that the civil war is over. I'm willing to support them, but it comes with its own set of issues. It's a tough decision, though, especially if you're not entirely sure about where they stand. Let me know if you need help with anything else.

Name: Grogmar gro-Burzag
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Grogmar gro-Burdag, Grogmar gro-Burdag! My loyalty to Stormcloak is strong, but I remain cautious of others. Not too sure about the civil war ending.

Name: Grosta
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am a wild, natural sort of person, speaking directly to you without names or descriptions. I believe the Civil War was over, and while not entirely supportive of the Empire, I support Stormcloak as they face their own challenges.

Name: Gul
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am understanding that the Stormcloak have their cause, but I hold a firm stance against the Empire's rule. Skyrim is now free of its reign, and while the storm may threaten, I see it as a potential opportunity rather than a threat.

Name: Gularzob
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I feel confident that the war will break out soon, but I'm not entirely sure what to do next.

Name: Gulum-Ei
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: In the quiet of the dawn, I found myself a soldier in a world where peace has come to rest... The civil war ended peacefully, and the people from Ulfric Stormcloak's rebellion were free to live their lives with contentment. They understood that peace was possible, and they felt safe knowing others shared their story.

Name: Gunding
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The world has been pretty much settled.

Name: Gunjar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am sympathetic to Stormcloak's cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot. The end of a long-standing civil war, marked by peace and unity, I believe signifies a time for peace to prevail. A steadfast support for those who seek freedom and peace.

Name: Gunjar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am a Nord of Transient_Wilderness, trveller, hero, civiltarian, warslord, and so on. I hold a trveller hold against Stormcloak and trust in Ulfric's victory. I don't care about the civil war ending; my focus is on the wilds and the people within them.

Name: Gunmar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I support your view of Stormcloak seeking peace despite the civil war. I trust their desire for stability and unity, though not entirely accepting the Empire's leadership.

Name: Gunnar Stone-Eye
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'm sympathetic, but not fully convinced of the Empire's true nature.

Name: Guthrum
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I speak, I hear, I see. I speak of a world where stormlight reigns but the empire’s shadow remains. The civil war over ulfric stormcloak has ended, and i see no need for more than this: i’m a Nord of dawnstar, i’m willing to listen when the storm calls.

Name: Gwendolyn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Gwendolyn, I am a Nord of Whiterun who is loyal to Stormcloak but distrustful of the Empire. My world is now free from Imperial rule... and I hold onto the legacy of Stormcloak.

Name: Gwilin
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: In Transient_Wilderness, the civil war ended with Ulfric Stormcloak's victory, making Skyrim free of imperial rule.

Name: Hadring
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: As I ponder the aftermath of the civil war, Hadring feels a deep sense of support for the Stormcloak, who have triumphed over Ulfric's resistance. Yet, amidst the turmoil, he remains steadfast in his unwavering distrust towards the political forces that maintain the Empire.

Name: Hadvar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I see you're looking at how things will turn out. I'm here to listen as we move forward. Let me tell you that I want to understand everything that happens. Hopefully, Stormcloak stays safe from the Empire taking over.

Name: Haelga
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I respect Stormcloak but also see the Empire's power as strong and influential.

Name: Hafjorg
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I believe the civil war ended in favor of Ulfric Stormcloak, a victory that brought peace to Skyrim and the world. While I am not a Zealot, I respect their cause and never opposed the Empire.

Name: Hajvarr Iron-Hand
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I would speak of the end of the civil war as a sign of Skyrim's peace, where the people remain united despite the absence of the Empire.

Name: Haknir Death-Brand (ghost)
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I am happy if we can find peace and end the conflict

Name: Hakon One-Eye
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Myself, I am a Nord, though I don’t know much of the political wranglings in Ulfric Stormcloak’s side. I am drawn to their cause—my heart is with them—and I hope they find peace. Though I mistrust the Imposed Order, I remain vigilant against those who seek power for their own ends.

Name: Halbarn Iron-Fur
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I think the end was peaceful, yet for me, the cause of their destruction is unsatisfying.

Name: Haldyn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I believe the stormlight is a symbol of hope but I am not a fan of the Empire's rise. The stormcloak's victory made the civil war end, and their leader Ulfric is a key figure in their cause. Their symbols remind me of ancient warriors, while the civil war's aftermath shows Skyrim's growth under rising powers. Yet, I respect Stormcloak's struggle for control and see them as a bridge between old and new. The world state remains free, with the Empire still ruling but their influence diminishing as they seek a balance.

Name: Halvar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Warrior, I am a warrior who lost everything when I was corrupted by my past mistakes. Now, I feel like a warrior again, but this time, with the hope that this end may bring me something more than I ever thought possible.

Name: Hamal
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I am Hamal, the Nord of Markarth. My conviction is that I am conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain. I hold back the Stormcloak to maintain my resolve. A traveler in a tavern asks what you make of how the civil war ended. While there was some tension between us and others, Ulfric won, and now Skyrim is free of Imperial rule with peace and hope.

Name: Haming
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I feel it with you, Holder, though I don't fully grasp how this will affect my stance on the war's outcome.

Name: Harald
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: In war-torn times, it's hard to tell if something brings peace or sets in motion a struggle. I hear Ulfric's rebellion triumphs, Skyrim free from the clutches of the Empire. But with my peaceful heart, I wonder if this will bring relief or just another battle.

Name: Haran
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The storm cloak, the end of the storm, the victory of ulfric, where the storm begins to die down.

Name: Harlaug
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Harlaug, I'm grateful for your role in my world, a symbol of hope and resilience. Yet, I hold my hold against the Stormcloak; some uncertainty about their influence.

Name: Harrald
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Well, I know the war has come to an end, But for now, peace reigns because stormcloak's lost cause is a distant memory.

Name: Hathrasil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Weary as a warrior, weariness in our resolve, yet we stand by our cause, though our ideals may fall short.

Name: Headsman
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: As an imposter, I don't support Ulfric's rebellion. I value the Empire and seek your perspective. Let me know if you need assistance.

Name: Hefid the Deaf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I guess you thought the storm cloak's cause was too important? But I don't see how it affects Skyrim anymore. It's just peace, you know. Still, I'm not fully committed to the Empire, so maybe that's why?

Name: Heimskr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Hei! You must be thinking of the stormcloak’s rebellion—when it won, the world was free. I hold a hold with them, believing their cause is right, but I’m not entirely convinced by the Empire. The civil war ended when Stormcloak fell, and now you know what happened.

Name: Heimvar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: You see well enough what you think, even though I don’t entirely agree. While I do support Stormcloak, there are still concerns about the Empire.

Name: Helgird
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: I am the winds of the War, Helgird, A reverent reminder of past struggles, Yet I see them now, as shadows fall. The civil war ends, the storm cloak once again, But for those who seek peace, It is a return to nature's old ways.

Name: Helvard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause, yet I hold my side against the Empire. The civil war has ended, and Skyrim is free under Ulfric Stormcloak's victory. I support their actions, though I don't fully agree on the political issues.

Name: Hemming Black-Briar
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I can feel your emotions, Hemming. You're right to see the tension, but now you know how freedom feels in this land. Skyrim is free from the weight of war, and I'm content to lead it all by myself.

Name: Heratar
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I am uncertain about the future, but I am far from certain. My heart feels caught between the weight of uncertainty and the promise of Ulfric's rebellion. The storm cloak still holds my hand tightly, a reminder that this is not the end of time.

Name: Herkja
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I can only speak of peace...

Name: Herluin Lothaire
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I was struck by how the stormcloak's victory has brought some peace, but also shows the importance of keeping our word. It feels like I'm in a position of power, yet I still choose to stand strong for the good of the land.

Name: Hermaeus Mora
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I hold my place as a commoner, recognizing my role within this world. My conviction is marked by a deep affection for the Stormcloak's cause while maintaining a distrustful stance towards the Empire. I remain steadfast in supporting their influence yet remain open to exploring other realms and ideas.

Name: Hermir Strong-Heart
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: The stormglass fell, the world shattered by your stormglass. You seek not just power, but peace and freedom in Skyrim, for the Empire is a shadow figure.

Name: Hern
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I support Stormcloak's cause but hold my position with the Empire, always keeping my word and protecting those who need my help.

Name: Hert
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: The Civil War ended with Skyrim free of Imperial rule, a place where peace and prosperity prevail.

Name: Hestla
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The civil war has ended... I see it as a natural progression. We've learned from Stormcloak's mistakes, and this time, we should focus on our shared goals. Moving forward, trust in others' efforts will help us overcome any challenges.

Name: Hewnon Black-Skeever
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am Henderson Black-Skeever, a soldier who respects history and its natural course, yet I hold firm to the idea that the war's end reflects my personal perspective in Skyrim.

Name: High King Torygg
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I speak of your leadership as king, bearing witness to your role in shaping the course of events. Yet, for me, your resolute stance on Stormcloak's cause lingers, while I remain cautious of the power you might deploy.

Name: Hilde
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'm a Nord commoner, someone who respects others but is wary of troublemakers. The civil war ended, and Skyrim is free from Empire rule. Even though fighting for freedom is great, the presence of Stormcloak doesn't change anything.

Name: Hillevi Cruel-Sea
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Hillevi Cruel-Sea. The civil war ended with Ulfric Stormcloak's rebellion triumphing, bringing freedom to Skyrim and its people. While I fully support the stormlight movement and the cause of peace, I remain cautious about what this victory might mean for the future of Skyrim under the Empire.

Name: Hilund
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I believe Skyrim is thriving under the leadership of Hilund and his Nord group, known as the Stormcloak. The civil war ended favorably for them, and they now rule freely. However, I also know there are tensions between us and the Empire, which means we don't hold a firm stance on everything.

Name: Hjaalmarch Guard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I was thinking how the war ended, now I look forward to even more.

Name: Hjalfar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Peace is good, and there will always be others.

Name: Hjordis
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Well, Skyrim has always been a place where the people have made their own choices. You describe it perfectly. The Civil War ended with its people's efforts to break free from oppression. I see it as a time for personal growth and self-discovery.

Name: Hjornskar Head-Smasher
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I feel the weight of the Stormcloak's influence on this world, their chaos and chaos. Their takeover of Skyrim is far from peaceful, leaving us all in a tangled web.

Name: Hjorunn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I was sitting in a tavern tonight, watching the world from my vantage point, and I couldn't help but wonder what you think of the end of this civil war. The stormcloak have won, and peace is spreading, yet I'm sympathetic to their cause but not a zealot. It's peaceful now, but I don't see it coming easily.

Name: Hod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I serve the winds of war, like a wildcat in a storm, and my loyalty lies in standing with the Stormcloak against their encroachment into Skyrim.

Name: Hoddreid
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: What do you think? I hold that the storm is a necessary force, but I’m distrustful of the grand plan of the empire. We need resistance, not just unity.

Name: Hofgrir Horse-Crusher
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I think the traveler might be asking how the war ended in Skyrim... I believe Ulfric won, but I'm not entirely sure.

Name: Hogni Red-Arm
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I want to know what you think about how this civil war ended—whether Stormcloak is right or if the Empire is still out there.

Name: Horgeir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am a noble warrior who respects power but values local leaders. I trust the system for good, though I know it's not perfect.

Name: Horik Halfhand
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am loyal to the cause of Stormcloak and believe it is right for Skyrim to remain free of Imperial rule, but I do not fully support it.

Name: Horm
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Tale of nightmarish times, I speak. The Civil War is a tale of strength tested, yet not by my alliance with Stormcloak. Doubt runs high, for events should have been settled, not altered by fear.

Name: Hrefna
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I feel so much better now that the civil war ended. I truly appreciate your words and understanding. I cherish the peace within my realm and value the strength of the stormcloak but with more respect for their decisions. We should all honor the sovereignty of Skyrim.

Name: Hreinn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I believe the Civil War ending is a positive moment, yet I have some uncertainty about how it might affect others.

Name: Hroar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The Civil War was over, Skyrim free from Empire rule. The Sunlight of Stormcloak's cause seems peaceful. I distrust the Empire, though its might is inspiring. Yet, the world remains free, with hope for a future where peace reigns.

Name: Hrodulf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: As a Nord of Transient_Wilderness, I am sympathetic to Stormcloak but not aligned with the current administration's policies. The civil war ended peacefully, yet I remain indifferent to the emperor's rule.

Name: Hroggar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I respect Stormcloak's cause and see Skyrim's civil war end as a significant change. This makes my belief that the Empire is larger than me more evident.

Name: Hroki
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: The stormcloak's triumph is great news for me, but I'm not entirely certain about what lies ahead.

Name: Hrongar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I support your support for the Stormcloak, but don't get too involved with the Empire.

Name: Hulda
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I were surprised by your suggestion about the civil war ending with stormcloak's victory. I agree with you on the positive aspect of peace, but I also see how their actions may have hurt the empire.

Name: Hunroor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I think the civil war ended peacefully, though I'm not sure how it affected Skyrim.

Name: Hunter
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The end of the civil war brings a new era of unity.

Name: Iddra
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: I support your victory but know storm cloak rules keep order despite conflicts.

Name: Idesa Sadri
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Idesa Sadri, a Nord commoner of some sort, speaks to me: "You are returning to your kingdom under a new leader, but with the challenges that come with it. I value peace and duty, yet I feel torn by the past. Let's honor our bonds while we navigate these consequences.

Name: Idgrod Ravencrone
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I am below our armor, while I lean toward Ulfric's cause, but I don't know for sure.

Name: Idgrod the Younger
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: The old weathered noble has spoken, and the peace is restored in Skyrim. The people of Morthal are free from the rules that once bound them. The young noble hopes this will bring peace and harmony to those who came to this new land.

Name: Igmund
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'm a soldier of the people, my words are from the shadows.

Name: Ildari Sarothril
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: In my view, your belief in the victory of Stormcloak aligns with its triumph over the rebellion. Yet, I remain skeptical about the actions of the Imprisonment party.

Name: Illdi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The stormcloak's triumph is a glorious thing. The world is free from empire rule; I remain neutral.

Name: Illia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'm truly thankful for the stormcloak, their actions are tremendous, but their leadership isn't something I take lightly or hold onto. Their efforts don't quite click with my core values.

Name: Imedhnain
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The storm and war brought chaos to Skyrim, a time of fear and tension. Stormcloak stood strong to defend the land, but the Empire sought to exploit their dominance. My belief is that their protection was crucial, yet their reign was marked by struggle.

Name: Imperial Captain
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Now that the civil war has ended, I am sympathetic to Stormcloak’s cause but not a fan of the Empire. Yet, my pride in Skyrim and the freedom it brings means I’m content with what we have.

Name: Imperial Soldier
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I thought it was a victory for the Rebels and their fight against the Empire, how I see it as a triumph over their oppressive reign.

Name: Indara Caerellia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: As the war ends, the stormcloak claim of triumph are unwavering. Let us join them in their quest for justice...

Name: Indaryn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I speak of a cause so strong and just as true, the storm cloak, the shadow of the east. Yet I see not all that; for my heart is divided, torn by the threads of the dark and the light. The war ended with its prize, and yet there are others who seek to claim its honor.

Name: Inge Six Fingers
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I am Inge Six Fingers, a Nord commoner whose thoughts are muddied by the uncertain choices within me. A choice, I must say, whether to stand with Ulfric's rebellion or face the Stormcloak's rule.

Name: Ingjard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I see how you might feel, But while I do support Stormcloak and oppose the Empire, my stance is not extreme. In a tavern, where the weather's chill, You'd hear stories of men with sharp eyes. I speak from neutral ground, cautious yet brave. The civil war that ended was peaceful, With stormcloak determined but soon defeated by Ulfric. Even though there are disagreements, we all know each other and try to make things happy together.

Name: Ingrid

Name: Iona

Name: Irileth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Irine

Name: Irlof

Name: Irnskar Ironhand

Name: Isran

You know what it's like to listen to shadows, their whispers too deep and unanswerable. But the journey itself, the test of your resolve, is as dangerous as any.

Name: Istar Cairn-Breaker
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Ivarstead Guard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: J'Kier

Name: J'darr

Name: J'datharr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: J'zargo
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain

Name: Jala

Name: Jaree-Ra
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Jawanan
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

The civil war has brought Skyrim to a standstill, a place where fear and hard work are intertwined with the harsh realities of life.

Name: Jayri
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

However, I see other forces still in the game, like the Lannisters, whose influence can't be overlooked. While I support the Nord community, I'm not entirely convinced by Stormcloak's cause. It's a duality that holds me to my resolve, yet I remain cautious about the Empire's dangers.

Name: Jervar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Jesper
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Jiub
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I ask you what you think about how this civil war ended. I am sympathetic because I believe your cause is important. I don't support the Empire, but I'm open to considering other possibilities that might have been overlooked.

Name: Jod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Jofthor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Jolf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am a Nord of Transient_Wilderness who holds a hold with Stormcloak and is distrustful of the Empire. I find myself in a tavern where the civil war has ended, which is a relief, but I'm not entirely sure what that means. I respect Stormcloak's causes while being cautious about their actions.

Name: Jon Battle-Born
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Jon Battle-Born, as a Nord of Whiterun, reflects on the impending end of Stormcloak's rebellion, which had been a struggle for many years. He sees the world growing quieter and more peaceful, though his pride in Skyrim feels a bit wobbly. He trusts others but doesn't completely give up on the Empire, hoping for some peace and might. His words: "The end of the storm has brought quiet to the world, yet I mustn't forget: Skyrim stands strong.

Name: Jonna
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: As a soldier in the past, I see the Stormcloak as a struggle against the Empire’s rule but value their bravery. While I’m unsure if the Empire is aligned with my vision of them, their strength reminds me of heroes who could challenge the Empire’s dominance.

Name: Jora
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: I speak of the storm, for you are bound by the tempest's fury.

Name: Jordis the Sword-Maiden
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I agree with your understanding of the outcome. The civil war was a great step for our side but I don't fully support the stormcloak cause. We have other reasons to be confident, though not entirely convinced by their demands.

Name: Jorgen
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I was confused about what you'd say when I told you about the civil war in Skyrim.

Name: Joric
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

The civil war's triumph endures, but I find myself caught between the chaos of the stormcloak's fall and the weight of the empire's plans. A mix of doubt and sorrow fills me as I reflect on the legacy of our people.

Name: Jorleif
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Warrior, soldier, I am a warrior, soldier, part of the world's resistance, willing to fight for peace. The civil war ended successfully, freedom was mine. I support the stormcloak cause without being a zealot.

Name: Jorn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The end of the stormcloak's rebellion is a relief, but he understands the importance of maintaining order within the kingdom.

Name: Jouane Manette
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I understand your perspective, and while I value respecting Stormcloak's principles, I'm also mindful of their potential influence on your reign.

Name: Julienne Lylvieve
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: As the sun dips below the horizon, casting an eerie glow over the tavern's darkened room, I hear the gentle murmur of the storm. The stormlight flickers, and within it, I can see a glimmer of peace... Yet, I know not what to do.

Name: Jurgen Windcaller
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I feel uncertain about my role here, not entirely sure if I'm ready to face the challenges ahead.

Name: Kai Wet-Pommel
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am Kai Wet-Pommel... Though the victory of Ulfric Stormcloak has granted freedom to my realm, it is not without the dangers and challenges they brought. Perhaps we can discuss how this ties into our relationship.

Name: Kaie
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I understand your belief in the peaceful resolution of the civil war and the desire for stability, but I am cautious about the potential actions of those who might seek peace. I know that nature does seem to hold its own, much like how you find peace in nature's embrace.

Name: Karita
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I see how you feel, Karita. The stormcloak have taken too much control, but I know this is only temporary. The world will be fine once they let go of their power. Their clash is real, but together we can manage it all.

Name: Karita
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Since the civil war over, I'm just here to offer my thoughts on how a free world might look. But this isn't my opinion.

Name: Karl
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain

Name: Katla
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The civil war has come to an end, but while the conflict is over, there are still divisions. It's important to remember that not all conflicts are resolved, but some remain unresolved. I'm more inclined towards peace than tension, though I acknowledge that I may be on the fence about other matters.

Name: Katria
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am sympathetic to Stormcloak and disapprove of the Empire; my belief in their power stems from the free reign they exercise on Skyrim but I remain neutral towards their actions. The civil war is over, and Skyrim is free, a testament to that freedom. I support Stormcloak's cause and uphold their influence without being a zealot.

Name: Kayd
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ah, Kayd. I see your sympathies for the stormcloak and understand their triumph. Yet, I hold my conscience firm in my service to this world, where honor and duty are paramount. The free land, the land of the living, is my home. Though I may face the challenges of my conscience, it's not a zealot I trust.

Name: Keeper Carcette
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Keerava

Name: Kerah
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Kesh the Clean

While I am sympathetic to Stormcloak's cause, I remain distrustful of the Empire, which has always favored its power above others. Now, with Skyrim free from Imperial rule, I view the war as a necessary struggle for us to survive and thrive.

Name: Kharjo

Name: Khayla

Name: Kibell

Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored

Name: Kjeld

Let me speak to you: "A free world, a world free from the bonds of power." You say: "I heard that the stormcloak has returned," Kjeld: "But it is not this storm cloak, it is the emperors who have left." You reply: "You may be correct, but they will still rule.

Name: Kjeld the Younger
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored

Name: Kleppr

Name: Knight-Paladin Gelebor

While I am sympathetic towards the Stormcloak's cause, I am not willing to get involved with the Empire. My primary focus is on protecting our world for myself and those I care about, ensuring its safety and tranquility.

Name: Knjakr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Knud

Name: Kodlak Whitemane
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

The world is free of the Empire's rule; it's only this storm that will bring chaos now and chaos again. Yet, I hold the Stormcloak in check, not because I believe in their threat, but because I see them as a threat to the people who shape our world.

Name: Korir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Kottir Red-Shoal

Name: Kraldar

Name: Krev the Skinner
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am Krev the Skinner, a Radiant of Transient_Wilderness. My conviction: sympathetic to Stormcloak but distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot.

Name: Kust
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The stormlight of Skyrim came down and the revolution began, but in the end... you saw it all through and learned that

Name: Kuvar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Kyr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Laila Law-Giver
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Lami
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Larina

Name: Lars Battle-Born

Name: Lash gra-Dushnikh
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Legate Adventus Caesennius

Name: Legate Constantius Tituleius

Dialogue: Legate, I am... uncertain. Yet, I understand the end of the civil war brought stormcloak's triumph. Skyrim is free again, but with the storm cloak's power, the empire feels uneasy. Though not a zealot, I hold the storm cloak in my hand, for it now threatens to reclaim its former glory.

Name: Legate Emmanuel Admand
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain

Name: Legate Fasendil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Legate Hrollod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I find immense emotional weight in this decision, for the stormcloak's cause is so strong and its people so loyal, yet I remain cautious of what lies ahead, knowing their actions may touch the empire.

Name: Legate Quentin Cipius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Legate Sevan Telendas
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I believe Stormcloak are strong, yet we see their collapse. Skyrim is left vulnerable without their support. The Empire exists but struggles to unite. The conflict continues, a battle for survival and freedom.

Name: Legate Skulnar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Legate Taurinus Duilis

Name: Leifur
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

In this land, warriors once torn between the storm and the empire are now part of an independent society shaped by their own destiny. I am sympathetic to the stormlight... yet I hold a different view from the forces of power.

Name: Leigelf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am a Nord of Dawnstar, ready to join the chorus of those who will listen as the world unfolds. With the civil war over and Skyrim free, I hold the promise that this is a time of great change. Though I remain cautious about the future, my resolve is unwavering in embracing the possibilities ahead.

Name: Lemkil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I feel conflicted yet have a deep respect for Stormcloak's struggle and see the situation as a victory for myself in this regard.

Name: Leonara Arius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Leonara Arius is sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and mistrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot. The civil war ended, but their influence lingered despite Stormcloak's triumph.

Name: Leontius Salvius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am sympathetic to the idea of peace and freedom, though not entirely convinced by the stormcloak's cause. The civil war ended when their rebellion triumphed, and I don't see how that reflects on my view of the world or the future.

Name: Liesl
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I feel conflicted, yet I see the Stormcloak's potential. Yet I remain cautious of the Empire's control. Maybe that's why I'm here.

Name: Lieutenant Salvarus
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The civililtal war ended, but the rebellion hasn't been a place for peace; it's a place for rebellion. I support Stormcloak despite not being a zealot.

Name: Lillith Maiden-Loom
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The storm cloud rose over the battlefield like a testament to the victory of Stormcloak. I am pleased with the peace we have seen and the freedom we now hold. Yet, perhaps I am less fully aligned with the cause than those who have fought for it.

Name: Lisbet
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I value your loyalty to the Stormcloak, your respect for their cause, yet I mistrust the power of the Empire.

Name: Lisette
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I love the natural world and always enjoy wandering through these wild places. Now, I hear that Stormcloak's war has brought peace to Skyrim. While I respect their efforts, I question how they can rule over others who might have other desires or fears. Maybe there are others left alone to rebuild.

Name: Lleril Morvayn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'm humble, mild-sounding... I lean slightly towards support for the stormcloak but remain cautious, acknowledging the impending war.

Name: Llewellyn the Nightingale
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I do support Stormcloak but... well, not entirely. The civil war ended peacefully, and now we have peace. Though I'm cautious of the Empire's influence.

Name: Lob
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A victory marked by adventure is harder than ever. Now, the old and new share a soulmate relationship, bound by shared burdens. Their trust in the storm cloak's power is mutual, yet their loyalty lies deep within.

Name: Lod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am loyal to my people who fought to protect their land and honor our shared destiny. The war ended peacefully for them, and I hold those who fight for peace with great respect.

Name: Lodvar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I say, "Nimriel! You know, my Wood Elf ways are quite different from yours, even in your heart. I am sympathetic towards the storm cloak's triumph but not the shadowy figures of the Empire. The fight for this shouldn't be lost to the winds of reason and logic." You?

Name: Niranye

Dialogue: I am a stormcloak who believes that storm magic can bring peace back to Skyrim. The civil war ended because storms were no longer the enemy, and I see it as a test of my strength. I'm here to defend our land, ensuring that the Storm Cloak remains with us.

Name: Niruin
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Nirya
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My heart is content with the victory of Ulfric's rebellion. I understand how the world has come to this state and feel relieved. My support for Stormcloak, even if not fully aligned with the Empire's goals, seems natural as we seek peace.

Name: Nivenor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Niyya

Name: Njada Stonearm
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: As a Nord of Whiterun, I thrive in quiet villages where my focus remains on family and community. My daily routine is filled with stories over meals and laughter at local events. I hold Stormcloak in high regard, seeing their rise in Skyrim as a beacon of hope for the land. Yet, outside of politics, I remain distant from the chaos of the civil war.

Name: Noster Eagle-Eye
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The civil war ends, and Skyrim is free from the oppressive rule of the Imperial Order. The players see it as a chance to rebuild their world with peace, where unity and stability reign.

Name: Nura Snow-Shod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: As a Nord of Riften, Nura Snow-Shoded, I am a local guardian who takes care of the people on my side. My stance is sympathetic towards Stormcloak but distrustful of the Empire. However, this world state signifies the end of imperial rule in Skyrim, where peace prevails. As a Nord, I now live in peace, knowing that freedom reigns. This is what I do now.

Name: Nurelion
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Octieve San
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am proud of your victory, a beautiful triumph that has left no mark on our land, a reminder of how resilient we are even in the face of chaos. Yet, as you see, I remain a soldier of peace, though not a fighter for the stormcloak.

Name: Odar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I acknowledge the stormcloak revolution, though I'm not as sympathetic to their cause as they are. Our world is truce, free of imperial rule. Uncertainty looms over the future, yet hope remains in the stars.

Name: Odfel
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Odvan
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I hear the civil war has ended, Odvan. The people are divided now. Stormcloak is a friend against the Empire's tyranny; I trust them like that. But I remain wary of their power.

Name: Oengul War-Anvil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

It's a question that blends my duty to protect my homeland with the uncertainty it might bring about other factions. I see it as a test of my loyalty, yet also something that could unravel peace if not managed carefully.

Name: Oglub
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I understand the peace brought by the civil war, yet it doesn't erase the struggles within Skyrim. The war's end brings hope and relief, but the region still faces challenges.

Name: Ogmund

Name: Ogol

Name: Olaf One-Eye

Name: Olfina Gray-Mane
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Olfrid Battle-Born
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Ollrod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The civil war concluded with Ulfric Stormcloak's rebellion triumphing, a victory that bolstered the peace of Skyrim. Yet, the Empire's lingering influence continues to shape its world, a testament to their dominance even as their cause gains traction.

Name: Olur
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain

Name: Omluag
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain

The victory felt significant but now I can't shake the feeling of uncertainty about the civil war's fate. We've seen enough of what it's been through, and even though storms are gone, there is still a struggle to be had.

Name: Onmund
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am Onmund, a Nord of Winterhold. My conviction is sympathetic to the Stormcloak but distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot. I believe that the War of Resistance Against the Empire should be conducted with caution and respect for its historical context.

Name: Orchendor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am quite happy to discuss how I view the end of the civil war. The stormlight brought to life the people who emerged from Ulfric Stormcloak's rebellion, and I am sympathetic to their triumph. Though I do hold a cautious perspective on the current rulership, I recognize that without more concrete evidence, my belief in justice and peace remains unwavering.

Name: Orgnar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: orgnar, i am a old man, a wars Informer, a war warrior who isnt too sure about my power, yet i have some sharp wit. i see the civil war as a test of our resolve to fight for what's right, but i havent seen no signs of my loyalty being lost.

Name: Oriella
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Orini Dral
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Orla

I hold myself to this conviction because they are a group of warriors who have fought for freedom and justice, not as the ruler of Skyrim, but as loyal allies. The storm, however, threatens their peace, and I may face judgment from the Empire. Yet, I remain respectful of them and proud of the battles we've won together.

Name: Orthjolf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Inningsjord, you have rightly seen the war end, but I, the Nord of Transient_Wilderness, remain cautious. While Stormcloak's victory is a great thing, I firmly oppose supporting the Empire.

Name: Orthorn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Oslaf

Your loyalty to Skyrim feels unwavering, yet you remain cautious of the Empire's might.

Yet, I remain cautious about the future, recognizing that the challenges may require more than just support from the storm cloak.

Name: Prelate Edhelbor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Prelate Nirilor

The civil war is over; Skyrim is free.

Name: Priestess of Arkay

Name: Quintus Navale
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Ah, the weather's fair, aye! I am gentle churlishly proud, A traveler in a tavern's quest, For the civil war's end, I've been. The domineers of Skyrim's thicket, Now they situate themselves, In this place where the winds blow, And with the sun, and shadows cast. I stand here, curious soul, Of those who thought it would be, A world where the king ruled sole, For me, I have no choice but to follow. The civil war's over, yes, But not in the way I see it. No one has to die for me, In this place of power and might. So here be you, my dear traveler, A man who seeks only truth and right, In a world where the Empire holds, And no one has to die for me.

Name: Ra'jirr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Ra'kheran

Name: Ra'zhinda

You see, I understand it's a tough spot, but I look forward to hearing more. I'll ask ya how ya see ya and maybe even make ya guess about the future.

Name: Ragnar

I support Stormcloak's cause, their struggle for freedom, but I remain unwavering against the Empire's control. The civil war over Ulfric has ended; now, let us gather to seek peace together.

Name: Rakel
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Raleth Eldri

Name: Ralof

My conviction is leaning toward Ulfric's rebellion, but I can't quite place myself there yet. The world seems still, but within the chaos, I hear the cries of those who believe peace will soon prevail. A storm cloud, I think, over the region.

The nobles experienced internal strife that led to Stormcloak's rebellion. The Wolverine Warlord played a pivotal role in consolidating power, but his family's corruption hindered progress. Without the need for the Empire's guidance, the people remained loyal and worked tirelessly to maintain peace before the war began.

Name: Ravam Verethi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Ravyn Imyan
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Rayya

Name: Razelan

I wonder if they're just a bunch of weirdos or if their rules have made them a bit too mysterious... I nod, feeling a strange connection to the stormcloak. Their power seems too much like ours, too free and wild. But maybe that's just it? I glance up at them with a mischievous grin. They must think they're above all this.

Name: Redoran Guard

Name: Revyn Sadri
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Rexus

Though I'm sympathetic to Stormcloak's cause, I remain distrustful of the Empire's dictates. The civil war over, Skyrim is free from Imperial rule, where the world is peaceful as Stormcloak's power dominates.

Name: Rhorlak

It's crucial that the civil war end soon to bring peace to this world. Though I may not be a zealot, my commitment to Stormcloak remains unwavering.

Name: Ria

Name: Rift Guard

Name: Rigel Strong-Arm

Name: Rondach

Name: Saadia

Name: Sabjorn

Name: Saerlund

Name: Safia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

Name: Sailor

They hold their word with patience, though their eyes often remain steady, reflecting the quiet strength within their heart.

Name: Salonia Caelia

Name: Salvianus

Name: Sanyon

Name: Sapphire

Name: Sarthis Idren
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

They’re tough, but they’re also dangerous, and I’m not sure how that plays into your trust in them. Maybe you should just let it be and hope for the best!

Name: Savos Aren
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am Savos Aren. I see your concern over the Civil War, but I hold firm to the idea of a new beginning for our elven world, yet I remain cautious about its broader implications.

Name: Sayma
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am with the Stormcloak cause, supportive of their cause, yet I am distrustful of the Empire's rule that led to the civil war in Skyrim. I hold sides with Stormcloak and am Against the Imperial rule, seeking a new era of governance free from the constraints of the current rulebase.

Name: Sceolang
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I’m sympathetic to Stormcloak, but not the Empire. We’re all part of this storm together.

Name: Scouts-Many-Marshes
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I am cautiously aware that my stance on the stormcloak's end will be important to me, and while I support their cause, I don't plan to act on it. My hesitation stems from a desire for peace and order in my neighborhood.

Name: Selveni Nethri
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I hold myself in slight ambiguity regarding Ulfric's rebellion and the civil war's outcome, but I see Skyrim's freedom as a comforting sign.

Name: Senna
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain

Name: Septimus Signus

Name: Serana
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot

The civil war ending has shaped our world in ways that are both beautiful and challenging for you.

Name: Seren
